file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module for HPID (HP ID) authentication operations. Provides utility methods for signing in, signing up, dismissing country selectors, confirming account verification codes, and completing the full sign-in and sign-up flows for HP ID authentication. Used throughout test flows to handle user authentication via HP ID system.

classes.class_purpose: Static helper class providing utility methods for HP ID authentication operations including sign-in, sign-up, country selector dismissal, and account verification code confirmation. All methods are static and operate on Playwright page objects to interact with the HPID authentication flow. Used as a reusable component across test flows requiring user authentication.
classes.how_to_instantiate: HPIDHelper is a static utility class - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the country selector popup appears

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to dismiss the country selector popup that may appear on the HPID page. Creates an HPIDPage instance with the provided page object. Tries to click the first country selector button using hpid_page.country_selector_button.first.click(). If the button is not found or any exception occurs during the click, the exception is caught and silently passed, allowing the flow to continue. This is a defensive method that handles the optional country selector popup without failing if it does not appear.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the HPID sign-in page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: User email address for HP ID authentication

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: User password for HP ID authentication

functions.what_it_does: Performs the HP ID sign-in flow by entering credentials and submitting the form. Creates an HPIDPage instance. First fills the username field with the provided email using hpid_page.username.fill(email). Then clicks the username form submit button using hpid_page.user_name_form_submit.click() to proceed to the password entry step. Next fills the password field with the provided password using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Finally clicks the sign-in button using hpid_page.sign_in.click() to complete the authentication. This is a two-step authentication flow where username and password are entered on separate screens.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the HPID authentication flow

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: User email address for HP ID sign-in

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: User password for HP ID sign-in

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete end-to-end HP ID sign-in flow including dismissing the country selector and clicking the sign-in option. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to handle any country selector popup. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the sign-in option button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.sign_in_option(timeout=20000), then clicks it. Finally calls HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password) to complete the credential entry and authentication. This is the high-level composite method that orchestrates the full sign-in flow.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the HPID sign-up page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address for the new HP ID account

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password for the new HP ID account

functions.what_it_does: Performs the HP ID account creation flow by filling the sign-up form with user details. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Fills the first name field with the hardcoded value 'InstantInk' using hpid_page.firstName.fill('InstantInk'). Fills the last name field with 'User' using hpid_page.lastName.fill('User'). Fills the email field with the provided email using hpid_page.email.fill(email). Fills the password field with the provided password using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Clicks the market checkbox using hpid_page.market.click() to accept marketing terms. Finally clicks the create account button using hpid_page.create_account_button.click() to submit the registration form.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the verification code entry page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address where the verification code was sent

functions.what_it_does: Handles the account verification code confirmation process with retry logic. Creates an HPIDPage instance and waits for the submit code button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.submit_code(timeout=20000). Sets up retry logic with 3 attempts and 10-second wait intervals. In each retry attempt, waits for the specified time using page.wait_for_timeout(), then fetches the verification code from the email using common.fetch_verification_code(email). If a verification code is retrieved, fills the code field using hpid_page.code.fill(verification_code), clicks the submit button using hpid_page.submit_code.click(), logs a success message with the email and environment details using framework_logger.info(), and breaks out of the retry loop. If an exception occurs during code retrieval or submission, logs a warning with the attempt number and exception details. If all retries fail and no verification code was obtained, logs an error and returns. Finally waits for the page to fully load using page.wait_for_load_state('load').

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the complete sign-up flow

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address for the new tenant account being created

functions.what_it_does: Orchestrates the complete end-to-end HP ID account creation and verification flow. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to handle any country selector popup. Creates an HPIDPage instance and waits for the create account button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.create_account_button_data(timeout=20000), then clicks it to navigate to the sign-up form. Calls HPIDHelper.sign_up(page, tenant_email, common.DEFAULT_PASSWORD) to fill and submit the registration form using the provided email and the default password from common module. Finally calls HPIDHelper.confirm_account_code(page, tenant_email) to handle the email verification code entry and confirmation. This is the high-level composite method for complete new user onboarding.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a well-structured helper module following the static utility pattern. All methods are static, requiring no class instantiation. The module provides both granular methods (sign_in, sign_up) and composite orchestration methods (complete_sign_in, complete_sign_up) that combine multiple steps. The confirm_account_code method includes robust retry logic for handling email verification code retrieval. The module integrates with HPIDPage for UI interactions, common module for shared utilities like DEFAULT_PASSWORD and fetch_verification_code, and framework_logger for logging. The dismiss_select_country method uses defensive exception handling to gracefully handle optional UI elements.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive helper module for landing page interactions and validations. Provides methods for opening and accessing the landing page, verifying RAF (Refer-a-Friend) banners and footnotes, validating page sections, testing navigation tabs, verifying hyperlinks in footer, testing cookie settings, validating FAQ links, selecting countries, and validating emergency banners with different color schemes. Supports both desktop (OOBE) and mobile (MOBE) viewport testing.

classes.class_purpose: Static helper class providing comprehensive utility methods for landing page testing and validation. Includes methods for page navigation, RAF banner verification, section visibility validation, OOBE and MOBE flow testing, hyperlink validation, cookie settings testing, FAQ validation, country selection, and emergency banner validation with multiple color schemes. All methods are static and designed to be called without instantiation.
classes.how_to_instantiate: LandingPageHelper is a static utility class - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like LandingPageHelper.open(page)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to navigate

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the Instant Ink landing page URL and waits for the page to fully load. Uses page.goto() with the URL from common._instantink_url and a 120-second timeout. After navigation, waits for the load state to complete using page.wait_for_load_state('load') to ensure all resources are loaded before proceeding.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to access the landing page

functions.what_it_does: Opens the landing page and handles initial page setup including privacy banner acceptance and optional banner dismissal. First calls LandingPageHelper.open(page) to navigate to the landing page. Creates a PrivacyBannerPage instance and calls accept_privacy_banner() to accept the privacy consent. Creates a LandingPage instance and attempts to click the close banner button using landing_page.close_banner.click() within a try-except block. If the close banner is not found or cannot be clicked, catches the exception and prints an error message. This method ensures the page is ready for interaction by handling common initial popups.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance where the RAF banner should be visible

parameters.name: expected_months
parameters.description: Number of free months expected to be displayed in the RAF banner text. Defaults to 1.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend (RAF) banner is visible and contains the expected text about free months. Creates a LandingPage instance and locates the RAF banner element using page.locator(landing_page.elements.raf_banner). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the banner is visible with a 30-second timeout. Retrieves the banner text content using raf_banner.text_content(). Constructs the expected text string as 'You have {expected_months} free month of HP Instant Ink'. Asserts that the expected text is present in the banner text, raising an assertion error with detailed message if not found. Logs a success message with checkmark using framework_logger.info() if validation passes. Returns True upon successful validation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to verify the RAF footnote

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend footnote element is visible on the landing page. Creates a LandingPage instance and locates the RAF footnote using page.locator(landing_page.elements.raf_footnote). Uses Playwright's expect() to assert that the footnote is visible with a 30-second timeout. This is a simple visibility check without text content validation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to evaluate viewport

parameters.name: section_selector
parameters.description: CSS selector string for the section element to check

functions.what_it_does: Verifies whether a page section is currently visible within the browser viewport using JavaScript evaluation. Uses page.evaluate() to execute JavaScript code in the browser context. The JavaScript function takes the section_selector as parameter, queries the DOM for the element using document.querySelector(), returns false if element not found. If element exists, gets its bounding rectangle using getBoundingClientRect() and checks if the element's top is at or below 0 and bottom is at or below the window's inner height, indicating the element is fully visible in the viewport. Returns the boolean result of the viewport check.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for desktop/OOBE testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots or performing actions at each step. Called with step name, page object, and screenshot_only flag.

functions.what_it_does: Comprehensive test method for validating all major sections and functionality of the consumer landing page in desktop/OOBE (Out of Box Experience) mode. Navigates to the staging landing page URL. Accepts privacy banner if present. Tests the Sign Up Now button by clicking it and verifying HPID page loads. Returns to landing page and systematically tests each navigation tab (How It Works, Instant Paper Plan, Plans, FAQs) by clicking the tab, waiting 5 seconds for scroll animation, verifying the section is visible, checking if section is in viewport using verify_section_is_in_viewport(), logging results, and optionally calling the callback function for screenshots. Wraps all operations in try-except block to catch and log any errors. This is a comprehensive end-to-end validation of the landing page user journey.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for mobile/MOBE testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots at each validation step

functions.what_it_does: Comprehensive test method for validating the consumer landing page in mobile/MOBE (Mobile Out of Box Experience) mode. Similar to consumer_landing_page_oobe but adapted for mobile viewport with hamburger menu navigation. Navigates to staging landing page, accepts privacy banner. Tests mobile menu button and Sign Up button. For each section (How It Works, Instant Paper, Plans, FAQs), opens the mobile menu, clicks the section tab, waits for scroll, verifies section visibility and viewport position, logs results, and calls optional callback. Uses mobile-specific locators like mobe_menu_button, mobe_sign_up_button, mobe_how_it_works_tab, etc. Wraps all operations in try-except for error handling.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for desktop validation

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshot capture

functions.what_it_does: Validates the 'Never worry about running out of ink again' section on the landing page in desktop/OOBE mode. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the never_worry_section element to be visible with 50-second timeout. Scrolls the section into view if needed using scroll_into_view_if_needed(). Logs success message. Calls optional callback function with 'never_worry' step name and page object for screenshot. Wraps operations in try-except block to catch and log any errors.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for mobile validation

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshot capture

functions.what_it_does: Validates the 'Never worry about running out of ink again' section on the landing page in mobile/MOBE mode. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the mobe_never_work_section element to be visible with 50-second timeout. Scrolls the section into view if needed. Logs success message. Calls optional callback function for screenshot. Wraps operations in try-except block for error handling. Uses mobile-specific locator mobe_never_work_section.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for desktop hyperlink testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for capturing screenshots of opened pages

functions.what_it_does: Comprehensive validation of all footer hyperlinks on the landing page in desktop mode. Creates a LandingPage instance. For each footer link (HP.com, Privacy Statement, Terms of Use, Cookie Settings, Your Privacy Choices, Instant Ink Terms of Service), waits for the link to be visible, scrolls it into view if needed, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab that opens when the link is clicked, waits for the new page to load, verifies the URL matches expected pattern using expect().to_have_url() with regex, adds time.sleep(5) for some pages to ensure full load for screenshots, logs success message, calls optional callback with page name and new page object for screenshot, then closes the new tab. Wraps all operations in try-except block to catch and log errors. This ensures all footer links navigate to correct destinations.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for mobile hyperlink testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for capturing screenshots

functions.what_it_does: Validates all footer hyperlinks on the landing page in mobile/MOBE mode. Similar to hyperlinks_validation but adapted for mobile viewport. For each footer link, waits for visibility, scrolls into view, captures new tab on click, sets mobile viewport size on new page using new_page.set_viewport_size({'width': 400, 'height': 740}), waits for load, verifies URL, logs success, calls callback, closes tab. Tests HP.com, Privacy Statement, Terms of Use, Cookie Settings, Your Privacy Choices, and Instant Ink Terms of Service links. Wraps in try-except for error handling.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for desktop cookie settings testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshot capture

functions.what_it_does: Tests the cookie settings functionality on the landing page in desktop/OOBE mode. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for footer cookies link to be visible. Uses page.expect_popup() to capture the new tab when clicking the cookies link. Waits for new page to load and verifies URL contains 'use-of-cookies.html'. Logs success. Scrolls the cookie preference button into view on the new page using new_page.locator() with the cookies_preference selector. Waits for button visibility and clicks it. Logs click success. Waits 5 seconds for cookie preference modal to appear. Gets the modal title text and verifies it equals 'Privacy Preference Center'. Logs title validation result. Waits for close button visibility, calls optional callback for screenshot, clicks close button, logs close success, and closes the new tab. Wraps all operations in try-except for error handling.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for mobile cookie settings testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshot capture

functions.what_it_does: Tests the cookie settings functionality on the landing page in mobile/MOBE mode. Similar to cookies_settings_oobe but sets mobile viewport size on the new page using new_page.set_viewport_size({'width': 400, 'height': 740}). Creates LandingPage instance, waits for cookies link, captures new tab on click, sets mobile viewport, waits for load, verifies URL, scrolls cookie preference button into view, clicks it, waits for modal, validates title, waits for close button, calls callback, clicks close, and closes tab. Wraps in try-except. Note: The code has a stray line 'expect(raf_footnote).to_be_visible(timeout=30000)' at the end which appears to be a copy-paste error and is not related to cookie settings.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for FAQ link validation

functions.what_it_does: Comprehensive validation of all FAQ section links on the landing page. Creates a LandingPage instance and stores the original URL. Scrolls FAQ section into view and verifies visibility. Defines four FAQ sections to test: General, Monthly plans, Yearly plans, and Pay As You Print plans with their corresponding section IDs. For each section, navigates back to original URL if needed, clicks the section tab, waits 500ms, checks for 'Show more' button and clicks if present, gets all question buttons in the section, iterates through each question, clicks it, waits for answer to be visible, finds all links in the answer, iterates through each link, skips mailto and tel links, clicks the link, waits 2 seconds, gets all browser pages, brings last page to front, waits for network idle, tries to verify HP branding image is visible or checks if URL contains '/terms', closes the new page, navigates back to original URL, re-expands the section and question to continue testing remaining links. Includes extensive error handling with try-except blocks and warning logs for failed links. Finally navigates back to original URL after all sections tested. This is a very complex method with nested loops and state management.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for country selection

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to select. Defaults to 'United States'.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a country from the country selector on the landing page. Creates a LandingPage instance and clicks the flag icon to open the country selector. Loads locale data using load_locale_data() to get country-to-language mappings. Retrieves the languages array and country code for the specified country from the locale map. Takes the first language from the languages array. Constructs a locale code string in format 'language-countrycode' (e.g., 'en-us'). Uses page.locator() with a data-testid attribute selector '[data-testid="{locale_code}-selector"]' to find and click the country option.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for emergency banner validation

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to determine expected banner color and icon. Defaults to 'United States'.

functions.what_it_does: Validates the emergency banner appearance including background color and icon based on country-specific configurations. Creates a LandingPage instance. Gets the parent element of the emergency banner using landing_page.emergency_banner.locator('..'). Retrieves font and style details using common.get_font_details(banner_element) and extracts the background-color value. Logs the detected color. Defines a country_configs dictionary mapping country names to their expected banner configurations including name, expected_color RGB value, and expected_icon_path SVG path string. Countries configured include Finland (Red Minus Banner), Bulgaria (Orange Alert Banner), Portugal (Green Done Banner), Australia (Blue Done Banner), Norway (White Done Banner), Poland (Grey Done Banner), and Estonia (Black Done Banner). Retrieves the config for the target country. Logs the test info. Asserts the detected color matches the expected color, raising assertion error with details if mismatch. Logs color validation success. If the config has an expected_icon_path, locates the SVG path element, gets its 'd' attribute, and asserts it matches the expected path, logging success. If no expected_icon_path (Bulgaria case), locates the orange banner icon element by class name and asserts it exists, logging success. Finally logs overall validation success message.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is an extremely comprehensive helper module with 14 static methods covering extensive landing page testing scenarios. The module supports both desktop (OOBE) and mobile (MOBE) testing with separate methods for each viewport. Key features include RAF banner validation, section visibility checks using JavaScript viewport evaluation, comprehensive hyperlink testing with new tab handling, cookie settings modal testing, complex FAQ link validation with nested loops and state management, country selection with locale data integration, and emergency banner validation with country-specific color and icon configurations. The validate_faq_links method is particularly complex with nested loops, dynamic element handling, and extensive error recovery. The module integrates with multiple page objects (LandingPage, PrivacyBannerPage, etc.), uses Playwright's expect assertions, includes optional callback support for screenshots, and has comprehensive logging throughout. The emergency banner validation includes detailed configurations for 7 different countries with specific RGB colors and SVG paths.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module for OSS (Online Setup Service) Emulator operations. Provides utility methods for accessing the OSS emulator, signing in, configuring printer details, selecting app types, starting flows, setting up OOBE and POOBE flows, accepting agreements, declining HP Plus, continuing through setup screens, selecting printer replacement options, activating HP Plus, selecting countries, and verifying reminder checkboxes and trial text. Used for simulating printer setup and enrollment flows in test environments.

classes.class_purpose: Static helper class providing utility methods for interacting with the OSS (Online Setup Service) Emulator. Includes methods for navigating to the emulator, signing in, filling printer configuration details, selecting app types, starting setup flows, configuring OOBE and POOBE flows, accepting agreements, handling HP Plus activation/decline, continuing through setup screens, and verifying UI elements. All methods are static and designed for use in printer setup and enrollment test scenarios.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OssEmulatorHelper is a static utility class - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to navigate to OSS emulator

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the OSS emulator URL and waits for the page to fully load. Uses page.goto() with the URL from common._oss_url. Waits for the load state to complete with a 60-second timeout using page.wait_for_load_state('load', timeout=60000).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for OSS emulator sign-in

functions.what_it_does: Performs sign-in to the OSS emulator by clicking the dev tab and then the HPID login button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the dev_tab element using oss_emulator_page.dev_tab.click(). Then clicks the oss_hpid_login button using oss_emulator_page.oss_hpid_login.click() to initiate the HPID authentication flow.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to click the 3-month button

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the 3-month Yeti button in the OSS emulator after a 5-second wait. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Waits 5 seconds using time.sleep(5) to allow the page to stabilize. Clicks the yeti_3m button with a 60-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.yeti_3m.click(timeout=60000).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for filling emulator form

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object containing entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, postcard (base64 encoded), and fingerprint (base64 encoded) attributes

functions.what_it_does: Fills the OSS emulator form with printer configuration details. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Fills the serial_number field with printer.entity_id using oss_emulator_page.serial_number.fill(). Fills the sku field with printer.model_number. Fills the uuid field with printer.device_uuid. Decodes the base64-encoded printer.postcard using base64.b64decode() and decodes to UTF-8 string, then fills the cloud_id field. Decodes the base64-encoded printer.fingerprint, converts to UTF-8 string, and fills the first fingerprint field using oss_emulator_page.fingerprint.first.fill(). Selects 'completed' option for language_config dropdown using oss_emulator_page.language_config.select_option('completed'). Selects 'completed' option for country_config dropdown.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for app type selection

parameters.name: app_type
parameters.description: App type to select (e.g., 'Gotham'). If None, uses common._simulator_platform or defaults to 'Gotham'.

functions.what_it_does: Selects the application type in the OSS emulator. Sets app_type to common._simulator_platform if app_type parameter is None, otherwise defaults to 'Gotham'. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the app_tab to navigate to the app configuration section using oss_emulator_page.app_tab.click(). Selects the specified app_type from the app_type dropdown using oss_emulator_page.app_type.select_option(app_type).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to start the emulator flow

functions.what_it_does: Starts the OSS emulator flow by clicking the start flow button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance and clicks the start_flow_button using oss_emulator_page.start_flow_button.click().

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for complete emulator setup

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object with configuration details

functions.what_it_does: Orchestrates the complete OSS emulator setup flow by calling multiple helper methods in sequence. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) to navigate to the emulator. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.sign_in_oss_emulator(page) to sign in. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.click_3m_button(page) to select the 3-month option. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator(page, printer) to fill printer details. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.select_app_type(page) to select the app type. Finally calls OssEmulatorHelper.start_oss_emulator(page) to start the flow. This is a high-level composite method for complete emulator setup.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for POOBE flow setup

functions.what_it_does: Configures the Post-OOBE (POOBE) flow settings in the OSS emulator. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the app_post_oobe_agreements_features option using oss_emulator_page.app_post_oobe_agreements_features.click(). Clicks the subscription_checkbox to enable subscription using oss_emulator_page.subscription_checkbox.click(). Clicks the oobe_checkbox to enable OOBE using oss_emulator_page.oobe_checkbox.click().

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for complete POOBE flow setup

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object with configuration details

functions.what_it_does: Orchestrates the complete OSS emulator setup for POOBE (Post-OOBE) flow. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) to navigate. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.sign_in_oss_emulator(page) to sign in. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.click_3m_button(page) to select 3-month option. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator(page, printer) to fill printer details. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.select_app_type(page) to select app type. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.setup_poobe_flow(page) to configure POOBE settings. Finally calls OssEmulatorHelper.start_oss_emulator(page) to start the flow. This is the high-level composite method for POOBE flow setup.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to accept printing services

functions.what_it_does: Accepts the connected printing services agreement. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Waits for the page to fully load with a 120-second timeout using page.wait_for_load_state('load', timeout=120000). Clicks the accept_all_button using oss_emulator_page.accept_all_button.click() to accept all connected printing services terms.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to decline HP Plus

functions.what_it_does: Declines the HP Plus offer during setup. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the last 'do not activate HP Plus' option with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.do_not_activate_hp_plus.last.click(timeout=120000). Then clicks the decline_hp_plus button using oss_emulator_page.decline_hp_plus.click() to confirm the decline action.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to continue past dynamic security notice

functions.what_it_does: Continues past the dynamic security notice screen. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance and clicks the ds_continue_button with a 200-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.ds_continue_button.click(timeout=200000) to proceed past the dynamic security notice.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to continue past value proposition

functions.what_it_does: Continues past the value proposition screen. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance and clicks the next_button with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.next_button.click(timeout=120000) to proceed past the value proposition screen.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to select printer replacement option

functions.what_it_does: Selects the replace printer option and continues. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the second replace_printer_radio_button using oss_emulator_page.replace_printer_radio_button.nth(1).click() to select the replace printer option. Then clicks the continue_button using oss_emulator_page.continue_button.click() to proceed.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to activate HP Plus

functions.what_it_does: Activates HP Plus during setup. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the Hp_Plus_Continue_button using oss_emulator_page.Hp_Plus_Continue_button.click() to proceed to the activation screen. Then clicks the Activate_HP_Plus button using oss_emulator_page.Activate_HP_Plus.click() to confirm HP Plus activation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for country selection

functions.what_it_does: Selects a country in the OSS emulator based on GlobalState configuration. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Locates the country element using page.locator() with an ID selector matching GlobalState.country_code. Wraps the selection logic in a try-except block. Scrolls the country element into view if needed using select_country.scroll_into_view_if_needed(). Checks if the country element is visible with a 100-second timeout. If visible, logs the country code using framework_logger.info(), clicks the country element, clicks the continue_selection button, and waits 10 seconds using time.sleep(10). If any exception occurs, silently passes to continue execution.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for verification

parameters.name: expected_months
parameters.description: Number of trial months expected to be displayed in the reminder text

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the reminder checkbox state and trial text content on the Instant Ink enrollment page. Locates the reminder checkbox using page.locator('[data-testid="instant-ink-reminder"]'). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the checkbox is visible with a 500-second timeout. Verifies the checkbox is checked with a 2-second timeout. Locates the reminder text element using page.locator('[class^="styles__CheckBoxText"]'). Uses expect().to_have_text() with a regex pattern to verify the text matches 'Remind me 2 weeks before my {expected_months}-month trial of HP Instant Ink expires.' where the non-breaking spaces are represented as \u00a0. Locates the trial info disclaimer using page.locator('[data-testid="general-monthly-fee-disclaimer"]'). Verifies it is visible and has the exact text 'After the trial, a monthly fee will be automatically charged unless canceled.'

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to access learn more

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the learn more button in the OSS emulator. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance and clicks the learn_more_button with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.learn_more_button.click(timeout=120000).

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper module provides comprehensive OSS emulator interaction methods with both granular actions (click_3m_button, fill_oss_emulator) and high-level composite flows (setup_oss_emulator, setup_oss_emulator_poobe_flow). The module handles printer configuration with base64 decoding for postcard and fingerprint data. It includes methods for both accepting and declining HP Plus, providing flexibility for different test scenarios. The country_select method uses GlobalState for dynamic country selection. The verify_reminder_checkbox_and_trial_text method includes detailed Playwright assertions for checkbox state and text content validation with regex patterns. The module integrates with OssEmulatorPage for UI interactions, common module for URLs, GlobalState for configuration, and framework_logger for logging.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module for Overview page operations and validations. Provides utility methods for verifying cartridge installation messages, checking printer groupings by status, validating plan suggestion banners, verifying plan upgrade information, checking unsubscribed status cards, testing HP Shop Ink links, enrolling printers again, and downloading past invoices. Used for comprehensive overview page testing and validation scenarios.

classes.class_purpose: Static helper class providing utility methods for Overview page testing and validation. Includes methods for verifying cartridge installation messages for different cartridge types, checking printer groupings by status, validating plan suggestion banners for upgrading or downgrading, verifying plan upgrade information, checking unsubscribed status card elements, testing HP Shop Ink link navigation, enrolling printers again after unsubscription, and downloading past invoices as PDF files. All methods are static and designed for comprehensive overview page validation.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OverviewHelper is a static utility class - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, 'k')

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for cartridge verification

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: Comma-separated string of cartridge types to verify (e.g., 'k', 'c,m,y', 'cmy')

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that specified cartridge types are installed and visible on the overview page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Splits the cartridge parameter by comma to get individual cartridge types. Defines a cartridge_locators dictionary mapping cartridge type codes ('k', 'c', 'm', 'y', 'cmy') to their corresponding selector strings from overview_page.get_selector(). For each cartridge type in the list, validates it exists in the dictionary, raises RuntimeError if invalid. Waits for the cartridge element to be visible with a 20-second timeout using page.wait_for_selector(). Asserts the element is visible, raising assertion error with cartridge type if not visible.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for verification

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: First cartridge type or combination to try

parameters.name: cartridge2
parameters.description: Second cartridge type or combination to try if first fails

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to verify cartridge installation with fallback logic. Wraps the call to OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge) in a try-except block. If a RuntimeError is raised (indicating the first cartridge type is invalid or not found), catches the exception and calls OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge2) to try the second cartridge type. This provides flexibility when the exact cartridge configuration is uncertain.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for printer section verification

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Group name or status text to identify the printer section (e.g., 'Active', 'Inactive')

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Expected number of printers - either 'only one' or 'more than one'

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the number of printers in a specific status group section on the overview page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Gets all printer status section elements using overview_page.printers_grouped_by_status.all(). Iterates through each section element to find the one whose inner text contains the specified group name. Stores the matching section in printers_grouped variable and breaks the loop. Asserts that printers_grouped is not None and is visible, raising error if section not found. Asserts the group name is in the section's inner text. Locates all printer selector options within the section using printers_grouped.locator(overview_page.printer_selector_option).all(). If number parameter is 'only one', asserts the array length equals 1. If number is 'more than one', asserts the array length is greater than 1. Raises exception if number parameter is neither of these values.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for banner verification

parameters.name: banner_type
parameters.description: Type of banner to verify - either 'downgrading' or 'upgrading'. Defaults to 'downgrading'.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the plan suggestion banner is visible and contains appropriate text based on banner type. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Gets the plan_suggestion_banner selector using overview_page.get_selector('plan_suggestion_banner'). Locates the banner element using page.locator(). Waits for the banner to be visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect() to verify visibility. Gets the banner text content and converts to lowercase. If banner_type is 'downgrading', defines a list of downgrade keywords ('downgrading', 'lower plan', 'reduce', 'save money', 'smaller plan') and asserts at least one keyword is in the banner text, raising detailed error if not found. If banner_type is 'upgrading', defines upgrade keywords ('upgrading', 'higher plan', 'more pages', 'increase', 'larger plan') and asserts at least one is present. Raises ValueError if banner_type is neither 'downgrading' nor 'upgrading'. Prints success message with checkmark and returns the banner locator.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for plan info verification

parameters.name: plan
parameters.description: Expected number of pages in the upgraded plan

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the plan information on the overview page shows the expected upgraded plan pages. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the plan_information element is visible with a 120-second timeout. Gets the inner text of the plan_information element and removes periods and commas using replace(). Extracts all numbers from the text using re.findall(). Takes the second number from the extracted list (index 1) as the plan pages value. Asserts the plan pages value equals the expected plan parameter, converting both to strings for comparison.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for unsubscribed status card verification

functions.what_it_does: Verifies all expected elements are visible on the unsubscribed status card. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses expect() to verify three elements are visible: unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button with 150-second timeout, unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button with 30-second timeout, and unsubscribed_download_past_invoices with 30-second timeout. This ensures all action buttons are present for an unsubscribed printer.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for HP Shop Ink link testing

functions.what_it_does: Tests the HP Shop Ink button on the unsubscribed status card by clicking it and verifying the opened page URL. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button is visible with 30-second timeout. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab that opens when the button is clicked. Clicks the button. Gets the new tab from the page info. Uses expect() to verify the new tab URL contains '/shop/pdp' with 30-second timeout using regex pattern. Closes the new tab.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for re-enrollment testing

functions.what_it_does: Tests the enroll printer again functionality by clicking the button and verifying the enrollment flow. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Clicks the unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new enrollment page that opens. Gets the new page and brings it to front. Wraps EnrollmentHelper.accept_automatic_printer_updates() call in try-except block to handle the automatic printer updates page if it appears, logging success or noting if page not found. Creates a ConfirmationPage instance with the enrollment page. Uses expect() to verify the v3_content_area is visible with 120-second timeout, confirming the enrollment flow reached the confirmation page. Closes the enrollment page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for invoice download

parameters.name: invoices_directory
parameters.description: Directory path where invoice PDF should be saved. Defaults to '/tmp/downloads'.

functions.what_it_does: Downloads past invoices as PDF from the unsubscribed status card and verifies the download. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices link is visible with 30-second timeout. Creates the invoices directory if it does not exist using os.makedirs() with exist_ok=True. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new page that opens when clicking the download link. Clicks the link. Gets the PDF page and brings it to front. Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(3). Constructs the PDF file path by joining the directory with 'invoice.pdf'. Saves the page as PDF using pdf_page.pdf(path=pdf_file). Closes the PDF page. Waits 2 seconds. Asserts the PDF file exists at the expected path, raising error if not found. Takes a screenshot of the main page and saves it to the invoices directory as 'invoice_pdf_file_screenshot.png'. Logs success message with the PDF file path.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper module provides comprehensive overview page validation methods with focus on cartridge verification, printer grouping, plan suggestion banners, and unsubscribed status card functionality. The verify_cartridge_installed_message method uses a dictionary mapping for flexible cartridge type validation. The verify_plan_suggestion_banner method includes keyword-based text validation for both downgrading and upgrading scenarios. The download_past_invoices_on_status_card method demonstrates file download handling with PDF generation, file system operations, and screenshot capture. The module integrates with OverviewPage, EnrollmentHelper, ConfirmationPage, and uses Playwright's expect assertions extensively. The click_enroll_printer_again_and_verify_enrollment method shows new tab handling and enrollment flow verification.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive helper module for Print History page operations and validations. Provides utility methods for verifying billing cycle periods, selecting billing cycle options, validating billing table data, normalizing currency values, validating currency fields, verifying billing section data with multiple fields, validating all billing cycles data, testing FAQ links and navigation, validating no-pens print history page state, downloading all invoices, checking notifications, verifying plan info, and verifying current total. Includes extensive currency normalization and validation logic.

classes.class_purpose: Static helper class providing comprehensive utility methods for Print History page testing and validation. Includes methods for billing cycle selection and verification, billing table data validation with currency normalization, FAQ link testing with navigation verification, no-pens state validation, invoice download testing, notification checking, plan info verification, and current total validation. Features extensive currency handling logic supporting multiple formats and locales. All methods are static and designed for thorough print history page validation.
classes.how_to_instantiate: PrintHistoryHelper is a static utility class - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like PrintHistoryHelper.verify_previous_billing_cycle_period(page)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for billing cycle verification

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that selecting a previous billing cycle changes the period title to not contain 'Current'. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Clicks the select_1_option to select the first previous billing cycle. Uses expect() with not_to_contain_text() to verify the billing_cycle_period_title does not contain the text 'Current', confirming a previous period was selected.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for billing cycle selection

parameters.name: period_index
parameters.description: Index of the billing period to select (1, 2, or 3)

functions.what_it_does: Selects a specific billing cycle period from the dropdown and waits for the table to update. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown and logs the action. Uses if-elif-else logic to click the appropriate option based on period_index: clicks select_1_option if period_index is 1, select_2_option if 2, select_3_option if 3, or raises ValueError if period_index is not 1, 2, or 3. Logs the selected period index. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_table_button is visible with 10-second timeout, ensuring the table has updated. Logs success message.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for billing table verification

parameters.name: total_pages_printed
parameters.description: Expected total number of pages printed to verify

functions.what_it_does: Validates the total pages printed value in the billing table. Logs the start of validation. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Gets the inner text of the total_pages_printed element. Extracts numbers from the text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(). Raises ValueError if no numbers are found in the text. Asserts the first extracted number equals the expected total_pages_printed parameter, raising detailed error message if mismatch.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for text extraction

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Either a locator object or a string selector

functions.docstring: Helper method to get text from a page element
functions.what_it_does: Utility method to extract text from a page element with flexible input handling. Checks if the selector parameter has an 'inner_text' attribute using hasattr(). If it does, treats it as a locator object and calls inner_text() directly. If not, treats it as a string selector, creates a locator using page.locator(), gets the first element, and calls inner_text(). Returns the extracted text string. This provides flexibility to accept both locator objects and selector strings.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Currency value to normalize - can be string, number, or None

functions.what_it_does: Normalizes currency values to a consistent format for comparison. Returns '0.00' if value is None or empty. Converts value to string, strips whitespace, replaces commas with periods (for European format), removes currency symbols ($, €, £), removes non-breaking spaces (\xa0) and regular spaces. If the normalized value is a dash, empty string, em dash (\u2014), 'N/A', or 'n/a', returns '0.00'. Wraps float conversion in try-except: attempts to convert to float, formats to 2 decimal places as string, and returns. If conversion fails, returns the normalized string as-is. This handles multiple currency formats, locales, and edge cases.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for currency field validation

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Locator object or string selector for the currency field

parameters.name: expected
parameters.description: Expected currency value - can be int (cents), float, or string

parameters.name: partial_match
parameters.description: If True, checks if expected value is contained in actual text. If False, requires exact match. Defaults to False.

functions.what_it_does: Validates a currency field value against an expected value with flexible matching. Gets the actual text from the selector using PrintHistoryHelper.get_text_from_selector(). If expected is an integer, divides by 100 to convert from cents to dollars. Normalizes the expected value using PrintHistoryHelper.normalize_currency_value(). If partial_match is True, replaces commas with periods in actual text and asserts the expected normalized value is contained in the actual text, raising detailed error if not found. If partial_match is False, normalizes the actual text and asserts it exactly equals the expected normalized value, raising detailed error with both raw and normalized values if mismatch. This supports both exact and partial currency validation with automatic normalization.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for billing section verification

parameters.name: expected_values
parameters.description: Dictionary containing expected values for billing fields like total_pages_printed, pages, rollover, additional, plan_price, overage_price, previous, charges, tax, total

functions.what_it_does: Comprehensive validation of all billing section fields based on provided expected values dictionary. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. For each field in expected_values, performs specific validation: For 'total_pages_printed', extracts numbers from the element text and asserts the first number matches expected. For 'pages', extracts numbers from plan_pages_text selector and asserts match. For 'rollover', skips if value is '-', otherwise extracts numbers from rollover_pages_text and asserts match. For 'additional', skips if value is '-', otherwise extracts numbers from additional_pages_text and asserts match. For 'plan_price', calls validate_currency_field with partial_match=True. For 'overage_price', calls validate_currency_field with overage_description_text and partial_match=True. For 'previous', calls validate_currency_field with previous_billing_text. For 'charges', calls validate_currency_field with current_billing_text. For 'tax', wraps in try-except, calls validate_currency_field with tax-amount selector if value is not '-', logs warning if element not found. For 'total', calls validate_currency_field with current_total_text. This method provides complete billing section validation with flexible field checking.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for multi-cycle validation

parameters.name: expected_data
parameters.description: List of dictionaries, each containing 'billing_cycle' key and expected values for that cycle

functions.what_it_does: Validates billing data across multiple billing cycles by iterating through expected data and selecting each cycle. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. For each row in expected_data list, converts the 'billing_cycle' value to 0-based index by subtracting 1. Logs the validation start for the cycle. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Logs the action. Locates all billing cycle options using page.locator() with the billing_cycle_options selector. Clicks the option at the calculated index using nth(). Logs the selection. Waits 1 second using page.wait_for_timeout(1000) for the table to update. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.verify_correct_billing_section() with the page and the current row data to validate all fields for that cycle. This enables comprehensive multi-cycle billing validation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for FAQ validation

functions.what_it_does: Comprehensive validation of all FAQ questions and their links on the print history page. Creates instances of PrintHistoryPage, OverviewPage, UpdatePlanPage, and DashboardSideMenuPage. For FAQ 1, clicks the question, verifies answer is visible, uses page.expect_popup() to capture new tab when clicking Terms of Service link, verifies URL contains '/terms', closes popup. For FAQ 2, clicks question, verifies answer visible, clicks overview link, verifies overview status card title is visible, navigates back to print history using side menu, clicks FAQ 2 again, clicks update plan link, verifies update plan page title is visible, navigates back to print history. For FAQ 3, clicks question and verifies answer visible. For FAQ 4, clicks question, verifies answer visible, clicks update plan link, verifies page title, navigates back to print history. This tests all FAQ interactions, link navigation, and page transitions.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for no-pens state validation

functions.what_it_does: Validates the print history page state when no pens/cartridges are enrolled. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify print_history_section and page_title are visible with 30-second timeout. Uses expect() with not_to_be_visible() to verify print_history_card_title, how_is_calculated_link, total_printed_pages, plan_pages_bar, and plan_details_card are NOT visible with 30-second timeout. This confirms the page shows the correct state for printers without pen/cartridge tracking.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for invoice download

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the download all invoices button with retry logic and verifies the invoices page opens. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Implements a retry loop with 60 attempts. In each attempt, wraps logic in try-except: clicks the print_history_table_button, uses expect() to verify download_all_invoices_button is visible with 5-second timeout, breaks loop if successful. If exception occurs and it's the 59th attempt (last retry), raises exception. Otherwise logs retry attempt number, waits 60 seconds, reloads the page, and verifies print_history_card_title is visible with 90-second timeout before next retry. After successful button visibility, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab when clicking download_all_invoices_button. Asserts 'invoices' is in the new tab URL, raising error with actual URL if not found. This provides robust invoice download with extensive retry logic.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for notification verification

parameters.name: notifications_text
parameters.description: Expected notification text to find in the print history table

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific notification text appears in the print history table. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Checks if the print_history_table is hidden using is_hidden(). If hidden, clicks the print_history_table_button to expand the table. Uses expect() to verify the table is visible with 30-second timeout. Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(3) for table content to load. Gets the table inner text using print_history_page.print_history_table.inner_text(). Asserts the notifications_text parameter is contained in the table content, raising detailed error with expected and actual content if not found.

functions.method_signature:
@staticmethod
    def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for plan info verification

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: Expected plan price value

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: Expected plan pages value

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the plan information displays correct price and pages values. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify print_history_card is visible with 90-second timeout. Gets the text content of plan_price_text element. Extracts numbers from the text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(). Asserts the first extracted number equals the expected plan_value parameter, raising error if mismatch. Asserts the second extracted number equals the expected plan_pages parameter, raising error if mismatch.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for total verification

parameters.name: total_value
parameters.description: Expected current total value

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the current total value displayed on the print history page. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify print_history_card is visible with 90-second timeout. Gets the text content of the first current_total_text element using print_history_page.current_total_text.first.text_content(). Extracts numbers from the text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(). Asserts the first extracted number equals the expected total_value parameter, raising error with expected and actual values if mismatch.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is an extremely comprehensive helper module with 13 methods covering all aspects of print history page validation. The module features sophisticated currency handling with normalize_currency_value and validate_currency_field methods that support multiple currency formats, locales, and edge cases including European comma-as-decimal format, various currency symbols, non-breaking spaces, and special characters. The verify_correct_billing_section method provides complete billing validation with conditional field checking. The verify_all_billing_cycles_data method enables multi-cycle validation by iterating through expected data. The click_download_all_invoices method includes extensive retry logic with 60 attempts, page reloads, and wait times. The validate_faq_on_print_and_payment_history method tests all FAQ interactions with link navigation and page transitions. The module integrates with PrintHistoryPage, DashboardSideMenuPage, OverviewPage, UpdatePlanPage, uses common module for number extraction, framework_logger for logging, and Playwright's expect assertions throughout.

key_reusable_methods: HPIDHelper.complete_sign_in — Complete HP ID sign-in flow with credentials
HPIDHelper.complete_sign_up — Complete HP ID account creation and verification
HPIDHelper.confirm_account_code — Handle email verification code with retry logic
LandingPageHelper.verify_raf_banner_with_free_months — Verify RAF banner with expected months
OssEmulatorHelper.setup_oss_emulator — Complete OSS emulator setup flow
OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator — Fill printer configuration with base64 decoding
OverviewHelper.verify_plan_suggestion_banner — Verify plan suggestion banner with keyword matching
